CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ship: Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# ROUTES.RB - API Endpoint Definitions
# =============================================================================
# This file maps URLs to controller actions.
# Run `bin/rails routes` to see the full routing table.
#
# URL Structure: /api/v1/auth/<action>
# - /api: Namespace for API (vs admin, webhooks, etc.)
# - /v1: API version (allows v2 later without breaking v1 clients)
# - /auth: Authentication resource

Rails.application.routes.draw do
  mount Rswag::Ui::Engine => '/api-docs'
  mount Rswag::Api::Engine => '/api-docs'
  # ===========================================================================
  # HEALTH CHECKS
  # ===========================================================================
  # These endpoints are used by:
  # - Docker/Kubernetes to check if the container is alive
  # - Load balancers to check if the server can handle requests
  # - Monitoring systems to track uptime

  # Basic liveness check: "Is Rails running?"
  # Returns 200 OK if the app is alive
  get 'up', to: 'health#show'

  # ===========================================================================
  # API V1 ROUTES
  # ===========================================================================
  # Namespace creates URL prefix AND module structure:
  # - URL: /api/v1/auth/login
  # - Controller: Api::V1::AuthenticationController#login

  namespace :api do
    namespace :v1 do
      # ========================================================================
      # AUTHENTICATION ENDPOINTS
      # ========================================================================
      # These are the core auth endpoints used by all clients
      # (frontend, mobile, other microservices)

      # POST /api/v1/auth/register
      # Create a new user account
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com", password: "securepass" }
      # Returns: { user: {...}, access_token: "...", refresh_token: "..." }
      post 'auth/register', to: 'authentication#register'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/login
      # Authenticate existing user
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com", password: "securepass" }
      # Returns: { user: {...}, access_token: "...", refresh_token: "..." }
      post 'auth/login', to: 'authentication#login'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/logout
      # Revoke refresh token (invalidate session)
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Body: { refresh_token: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Logged out successfully" }
      post 'auth/logout', to: 'authentication#logout'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
      # Exchange refresh token for new access token
      # Body: { refresh_token: "..." }
      # Returns: { access_token: "...", user: {...} }
      post 'auth/refresh', to: 'authentication#refresh'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/validate
      # Validate an access token (used by other microservices)
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Returns: { valid: true, payload: {...} } or { valid: false, error: "..." }
      # CRITICAL: Core API and Admin call this to verify tokens
      post 'auth/validate', to: 'authentication#validate'

      # GET /api/v1/auth/me
      # Get current authenticated user's info
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Returns: { user: {...} }
      get 'auth/me', to: 'authentication#me'

      # PATCH /api/v1/auth/password
      # Change authenticated user's password
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Body: { current_password: "...", new_password: "...", new_password_confirmation: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Password updated successfully" }
      patch 'auth/password', to: 'authentication#change_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
      # Request password reset email
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com" }
      # Returns: { message: "If an account exists..." } (always 200 for security)
      post 'auth/forgot-password', to: 'authentication#forgot_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
      # Reset password using token from email
      # Body: { token: "...", new_password: "...", new_password_confirmation: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Password reset successfully" }
      post 'auth/reset-password', to: 'authentication#reset_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email
      # Verify email address using token from verification email
      # Body: { token: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Email verified successfully" }
      post 'auth/verify-email', to: 'authentication#verify_email'
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTHENTICATION CONTROLLER
# =============================================================================
# Handles all authentication operations:
# - register: Create new user account
# - login: Authenticate and issue tokens
# - logout: Revoke refresh token and current access token (JTI)
# - refresh: Exchange refresh token for new access token
# - validate: Verify access token (for other microservices)
# - me: Get current user info
# - change_password: Change authenticated user's password
# - forgot_password: Request password reset email
# - reset_password: Reset password via token
# - verify_email: Confirm email address via token
#
# SECURITY PRINCIPLES:
# 1. Generic error messages (don't reveal if email exists)
# 2. Timing-safe comparisons (bcrypt handles this)
# 3. Rate limiting (handled by Rack::Attack middleware)
# 4. Token validation on protected endpoints
# 5. JTI-based access token revocation on logout and password changes

module API
  module V1
    class AuthenticationController < ApplicationController
      # ========================================================================
      # BEFORE ACTIONS
      # ========================================================================

      before_action :authenticate_request, only: %i[me logout change_password]

      # ========================================================================
      # ACTIONS
      # ========================================================================

      # POST /api/v1/auth/register
      def register
        credential = AuthCredential.new(register_params)

        if credential.save
          tokens = generate_tokens(credential)
          send_verification_email(credential)
          render json: {
            message: 'Account created successfully',
            user: user_payload(credential),
            access_token: tokens[:access_token],
            refresh_token: tokens[:refresh_token]
          }, status: :created
        else
          render json: { errors: credential.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/login
      def login
        credential = AuthCredential.find_by(email: params[:email]&.downcase&.strip)

        if credential&.authenticate(params[:password])
          tokens = generate_tokens(credential)
          render json: {
            message: 'Login successful',
            user: user_payload(credential),
            access_token: tokens[:access_token],
            refresh_token: tokens[:refresh_token]
          }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid email or password' }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/logout
      def logout
        refresh_token = RefreshToken.find_by(token: params[:refresh_token])

        if refresh_token&.auth_credential_id == @current_user.id
          refresh_token.revoke!
          # Revoke the current access token by JTI so it cannot be reused
          # even within its remaining 15-minute window.
          TokenDenylist.revoke!(@current_token_jti, @current_token_exp)
          render json: { message: 'Logged out successfully' }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid refresh token' }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
      def refresh
        refresh_token = RefreshToken.find_by(token: params[:refresh_token])

        if refresh_token&.valid_for_use?
          refresh_token.touch_last_used!
          credential = refresh_token.auth_credential
          access_token = JWTService.generate_access_token(credential)

          render json: {
            access_token: access_token,
            user: user_payload(credential)
          }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired refresh token' }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/validate
      def validate
        token = extract_token_from_header

        if token.blank?
          render json: { valid: false, error: 'No token provided' }, status: :unauthorized
          return
        end

        result = JWTService.decode(token)

        if result.success?
          render json: { valid: true, payload: result.payload }
        else
          render json: { valid: false, error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # GET /api/v1/auth/me
      def me
        render json: { user: user_payload(@current_user) }
      end

      # PATCH /api/v1/auth/password
      def change_password
        return render_current_password_error unless current_password_valid?
        return render_password_mismatch_error unless password_confirmation_matches?

        success = false
        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          success = @current_user.update(password: params[:new_password])
          @current_user.refresh_tokens.find_each(&:revoke!) if success
        end

        if success
          TokenDenylist.revoke!(@current_token_jti, @current_token_exp)
          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(@current_user.id)
          render json: { message: 'Password updated successfully' }
        else
          render json: { errors: @current_user.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
      def forgot_password
        credential = AuthCredential.find_by(email: params[:email]&.downcase&.strip)

        if credential
          # Invalidate any existing active reset tokens before issuing a new one.
          # Prevents an attacker from using an old token intercepted from a prior email.
          credential.password_reset_tokens.active.update_all(consumed_at: Time.current)
          reset_token = credential.password_reset_tokens.create!
          publish_password_reset_event(credential, reset_token)
        end

        render json: { message: 'If an account exists with this email, you will receive password reset instructions' }
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
      def reset_password
        @reset_token = PasswordResetToken.find_by(token: params[:token])

        return render_invalid_reset_token unless @reset_token&.valid_for_use?
        return render_password_mismatch_error unless reset_password_confirmation_matches?

        credential = @reset_token.auth_credential
        success = false

        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          success = credential.update(password: params[:new_password])
          if success
            @reset_token.consume!
            credential.refresh_tokens.active.find_each(&:revoke!)
          end
        end

        if success
          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(credential.id)
          render json: { message: 'Password reset successfully' }
        else
          render json: { errors: credential.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email
      def verify_email
        verification_token = EmailVerificationToken.find_by(token: params[:token])

        return render_invalid_verification_token unless verification_token&.valid_for_use?

        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          verification_token.auth_credential.update!(email_verified_at: Time.current)
          verification_token.consume!
        end

        render json: { message: 'Email verified successfully' }
      end

      private

      def current_password_valid?
        @current_user.authenticate(params[:current_password])
      end

      def password_confirmation_matches?
        params[:new_password] == params[:new_password_confirmation]
      end

      def reset_password_confirmation_matches?
        params[:new_password] == params[:new_password_confirmation]
      end

      def render_current_password_error
        render json: { error: 'Current password is incorrect' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def render_password_mismatch_error
        render json: { errors: ["Password confirmation doesn't match Password"] }, status: :unprocessable_content
      end

      def render_invalid_reset_token
        render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired reset token' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def render_invalid_verification_token
        render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired verification token' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def publish_password_reset_event(credential, reset_token)
        frontend_url = ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:5173')
        reset_url = "#{frontend_url}/reset-password?token=#{reset_token.token}"

        EventPublisher.publish('email.password_reset', build_reset_event(credential, reset_url))
      end

      def build_reset_event(credential, reset_url)
        {
          event_type: 'email.password_reset',
          timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
          payload: {
            to: credential.email,
            subject: 'Reset Your Password - ThatCookiesMine',
            data: { reset_url: reset_url, expires_in: '24 hours' }
          }
        }
      end

      def send_verification_email(credential)
        # Invalidate any prior unverified tokens before issuing a new one.
        credential.email_verification_tokens.active.update_all(consumed_at: Time.current)
        verification_token = credential.email_verification_tokens.create!
        frontend_url = ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:5173')
        verify_url = "#{frontend_url}/verify-email?token=#{verification_token.token}"

        EventPublisher.publish('email.verification', {
                                 event_type: 'email.verification',
                                 timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
                                 payload: {
                                   to: credential.email,
                                   subject: 'Verify Your Email - ThatCookiesMine',
                                   data: { verify_url: verify_url, expires_in: '24 hours' }
                                 }
                               })
      rescue StandardError => e
        # Verification email is best-effort; registration already succeeded.
        Rails.logger.error "[AuthenticationController] Failed to send verification email: #{e.message}"
      end

      def register_params
        params.permit(:email, :password)
      end

      def authenticate_request
        token = extract_token_from_header

        if token.blank?
          render json: { error: 'Authorization header missing' }, status: :unauthorized
          return
        end

        result = JWTService.decode(token)

        if result.success?
          @current_user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: result.payload[:sub])
          unless @current_user
            render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :unauthorized
            return
          end
          # Store JTI + exp so logout can revoke this specific token.
          @current_token_jti = result.payload[:jti]
          @current_token_exp = result.payload[:exp]
        else
          render json: { error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      def extract_token_from_header
        header = request.headers['Authorization']
        header&.split&.last
      end

      def generate_tokens(credential)
        {
          access_token: JWTService.generate_access_token(credential),
          refresh_token: credential.refresh_tokens.create!.token
        }
      end

      def user_payload(credential)
        {
          id: credential.id,
          email: credential.email,
          role: credential.role,
          email_verified: credential.email_verified?,
          created_at: credential.created_at

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTHENTICATION CONTROLLER
# =============================================================================
# Handles all authentication operations:
# - register: Create new user account
# - login: Authenticate and issue tokens
# - logout: Revoke refresh token and current access token (JTI)
# - refresh: Exchange refresh token for new access token
# - validate: Verify access token (for other microservices)
# - me: Get current user info
# - change_password: Change authenticated user's password
# - forgot_password: Request password reset email
# - reset_password: Reset password via token
# - verify_email: Confirm email address via token
#
# SECURITY PRINCIPLES:
# 1. Generic error messages (don't reveal if email exists)
# 2. Timing-safe comparisons (bcrypt handles this)
# 3. Rate limiting (handled by Rack::Attack middleware)
# 4. Token validation on protected endpoints
# 5. JTI-based access token revocation on logout and password changes

module API
  module V1
    class AuthenticationController < ApplicationController
      # ========================================================================
      # BEFORE ACTIONS
      # ========================================================================

      before_action :authenticate_request, only: %i[me logout change_password]

      # ========================================================================
      # ACTIONS
      # ========================================================================

      # POST /api/v1/auth/register
      def register
        credential = AuthCredential.new(register_params)

        if credential.save
          tokens = generate_tokens(credential)
          send_verification_email(credential)
          render json: {
            message: 'Account created successfully',
            user: user_payload(credential),
            access_token: tokens[:access_token],
            refresh_token: tokens[:refresh_token]
          }, status: :created
        else
          render json: { errors: credential.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/login
      def login
        credential = AuthCredential.find_by(email: params[:email]&.downcase&.strip)

        if credential&.authenticate(params[:password])
          tokens = generate_tokens(credential)
          render json: {
            message: 'Login successful',
            user: user_payload(credential),
            access_token: tokens[:access_token],
            refresh_token: tokens[:refresh_token]
          }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid email or password' }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/logout
      def logout
        refresh_token = RefreshToken.find_by(token: params[:refresh_token])

        if refresh_token&.auth_credential_id == @current_user.id
          refresh_token.revoke!
          # Revoke the current access token by JTI so it cannot be reused
          # even within its remaining 15-minute window.
          TokenDenylist.revoke!(@current_token_jti, @current_token_exp)
          render json: { message: 'Logged out successfully' }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid refresh token' }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
      def refresh
        refresh_token = RefreshToken.find_by(token: params[:refresh_token])

        if refresh_token&.valid_for_use?
          refresh_token.touch_last_used!
          credential = refresh_token.auth_credential
          access_token = JWTService.generate_access_token(credential)

          render json: {
            access_token: access_token,
            user: user_payload(credential)
          }
        else
          render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired refresh token' }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/validate
      def validate
        token = extract_token_from_header

        if token.blank?
          render json: { valid: false, error: 'No token provided' }, status: :unauthorized
          return
        end

        result = JWTService.decode(token)

        if result.success?
          render json: { valid: true, payload: result.payload }
        else
          render json: { valid: false, error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      # GET /api/v1/auth/me
      def me
        render json: { user: user_payload(@current_user) }
      end

      # PATCH /api/v1/auth/password
      def change_password
        return render_current_password_error unless current_password_valid?
        return render_password_mismatch_error unless password_confirmation_matches?

        success = false
        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          success = @current_user.update(password: params[:new_password])
          @current_user.refresh_tokens.find_each(&:revoke!) if success
        end

        if success
          TokenDenylist.revoke!(@current_token_jti, @current_token_exp)
          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(@current_user.id)
          render json: { message: 'Password updated successfully' }
        else
          render json: { errors: @current_user.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
      def forgot_password
        credential = AuthCredential.find_by(email: params[:email]&.downcase&.strip)

        if credential
          # Invalidate any existing active reset tokens before issuing a new one.
          # Prevents an attacker from using an old token intercepted from a prior email.
          credential.password_reset_tokens.active.update_all(consumed_at: Time.current)
          reset_token = credential.password_reset_tokens.create!
          publish_password_reset_event(credential, reset_token)
        end

        render json: { message: 'If an account exists with this email, you will receive password reset instructions' }
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
      def reset_password
        @reset_token = PasswordResetToken.find_by(token: params[:token])

        return render_invalid_reset_token unless @reset_token&.valid_for_use?
        return render_password_mismatch_error unless reset_password_confirmation_matches?

        credential = @reset_token.auth_credential
        success = false

        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          success = credential.update(password: params[:new_password])
          if success
            @reset_token.consume!
            credential.refresh_tokens.active.find_each(&:revoke!)
          end
        end

        if success
          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(credential.id)
          render json: { message: 'Password reset successfully' }
        else
          render json: { errors: credential.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_content
        end
      end

      # POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email
      def verify_email
        verification_token = EmailVerificationToken.find_by(token: params[:token])

        return render_invalid_verification_token unless verification_token&.valid_for_use?

        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
          verification_token.auth_credential.update!(email_verified_at: Time.current)
          verification_token.consume!
        end

        render json: { message: 'Email verified successfully' }
      end

      private

      def current_password_valid?
        @current_user.authenticate(params[:current_password])
      end

      def password_confirmation_matches?
        params[:new_password] == params[:new_password_confirmation]
      end

      def reset_password_confirmation_matches?
        params[:new_password] == params[:new_password_confirmation]
      end

      def render_current_password_error
        render json: { error: 'Current password is incorrect' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def render_password_mismatch_error
        render json: { errors: ["Password confirmation doesn't match Password"] }, status: :unprocessable_content
      end

      def render_invalid_reset_token
        render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired reset token' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def render_invalid_verification_token
        render json: { error: 'Invalid or expired verification token' }, status: :unauthorized
      end

      def publish_password_reset_event(credential, reset_token)
        frontend_url = ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:5173')
        reset_url = "#{frontend_url}/reset-password?token=#{reset_token.token}"

        EventPublisher.publish('email.password_reset', build_reset_event(credential, reset_url))
      end

      def build_reset_event(credential, reset_url)
        {
          event_type: 'email.password_reset',
          timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
          payload: {
            to: credential.email,
            subject: 'Reset Your Password - ThatCookiesMine',
            data: { reset_url: reset_url, expires_in: '24 hours' }
          }
        }
      end

      def send_verification_email(credential)
        # Invalidate any prior unverified tokens before issuing a new one.
        credential.email_verification_tokens.active.update_all(consumed_at: Time.current)
        verification_token = credential.email_verification_tokens.create!
        frontend_url = ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:5173')
        verify_url = "#{frontend_url}/verify-email?token=#{verification_token.token}"

        EventPublisher.publish('email.verification', {
                                 event_type: 'email.verification',
                                 timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
                                 payload: {
                                   to: credential.email,
                                   subject: 'Verify Your Email - ThatCookiesMine',
                                   data: { verify_url: verify_url, expires_in: '24 hours' }
                                 }
                               })
      rescue StandardError => e
        # Verification email is best-effort; registration already succeeded.
        Rails.logger.error "[AuthenticationController] Failed to send verification email: #{e.message}"
      end

      def register_params
        params.permit(:email, :password)
      end

      def authenticate_request
        token = extract_token_from_header

        if token.blank?
          render json: { error: 'Authorization header missing' }, status: :unauthorized
          return
        end

        result = JWTService.decode(token)

        if result.success?
          @current_user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: result.payload[:sub])
          unless @current_user
            render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :unauthorized
            return
          end
          # Store JTI + exp so logout can revoke this specific token.
          @current_token_jti = result.payload[:jti]
          @current_token_exp = result.payload[:exp]
        else
          render json: { error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
        end
      end

      def extract_token_from_header
        header = request.headers['Authorization']
        header&.split&.last
      end

      def generate_tokens(credential)
        {
          access_token: JWTService.generate_access_token(credential),
          refresh_token: credential.refresh_tokens.create!.token
        }
      end

      def user_payload(credential)
        {
          id: credential.id,
          email: credential.email,
          role: credential.role,
          email_verified: credential.email_verified?,
          created_at: credential.created_at
        }
      end
    end
  end
end

Good picture now. Here's the landscape:

**Done & solid:**
- `auth-api` — all 9 endpoints, JWT/refresh/revocation, HIBP, email verification
- `notification-service` — both `email.password_reset` + `email.verification` wired

**Broken end-to-end right now:**
- `auth-api` sends links to `FRONTEND_URL/reset-password?token=...` and `/verify-email?token=...` but `frontend/` is an empty scaffold. Those email flows are dead until those pages exist.

**Not started:**
- `core-api` — the actual business domain (appointments, patients, doctors, etc.)
- `admin` — only login + dashboard, no user management

---

**Recommended order:**

1. **`frontend/` — reset-password + verify-email pages** — unblocks the auth flows that are already built. Small scope, high value. Two pages: form to submit new password with token, button to consume verification token.

2. **`core-api/` — start the Go service** — appointments, patients, providers. This is the product. Auth calls `/api/v1/auth/validate` on every request — the infra hook is already there.

3. **`admin/` — user management** — table of auth credentials, ability to view/deactivate users. Depends on core-api or a direct admin endpoint.

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTH CREDENTIAL MODEL
# =============================================================================
# Represents a user's login credentials (email + password).
#
# This is the core model for authentication. It:
# - Stores email and hashed password
# - Validates password requirements (NIST SP 800-63B compliant)
# - Associates with refresh tokens for session management
# - Tracks password history to prevent reuse
#
# SECURITY: Passwords are NEVER stored in plain text.
# We use bcrypt (via has_secure_password) which:
# - Hashes the password with a random salt
# - Uses intentionally slow algorithm (prevents brute-force)
# - Automatically handles comparison securely
class AuthCredential < ApplicationRecord
  # ===========================================================================
  # CONSTANTS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Valid user roles for authorization
  # 'user': Regular patient/customer
  # 'admin': Administrator with elevated privileges
  ROLES = %w[user admin].freeze

  # ===========================================================================
  # SECURE PASSWORD
  # ===========================================================================
  # This Rails macro adds:
  # - password= setter that hashes the password
  # - password_confirmation= setter for confirmation validation
  # - authenticate(password) method for login verification
  # - Validation that password_digest is present
  #
  # Requires 'bcrypt' gem and password_digest column in database
  has_secure_password

  # ===========================================================================
  # ASSOCIATIONS
  # ===========================================================================

  # One user can have multiple refresh tokens (multiple devices/sessions)
  # dependent: :destroy → Delete all tokens when user is deleted
  has_many :refresh_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # Track password history to prevent reuse (NIST requirement)
  has_many :password_histories, dependent: :destroy

  # Password reset tokens for forgot password flow
  has_many :password_reset_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # Email verification tokens
  has_many :email_verification_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # ===========================================================================
  # VALIDATIONS
  # ===========================================================================
  # Validations run before saving to database.
  # Invalid records are not saved, and errors are added to the object.

  # Email must be present, unique, and valid format
  validates :email,
            presence: true,
            uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false },
            format: { with: URI::MailTo::EMAIL_REGEXP }

  # NIST SP 800-63B compliant password validation
  # Only validate when creating or changing password
  # Note: Use :: prefix to reference top-level PasswordValidator (not AuthCredential::PasswordValidator)
  validates_with ::PasswordValidator, if: -> { new_record? || password.present? }

  # Role must be present and one of the valid roles
  validates :role,
            presence: true,
            inclusion: { in: ROLES }

  # ===========================================================================
  # CALLBACKS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Record password in history after successful save (for reuse prevention)
  after_save :record_password_history, if: :saved_change_to_password_digest?

  # ===========================================================================
  # NORMALIZATIONS (Rails 7.1+)
  # ===========================================================================
  # Normalizations transform attribute values before validation/saving.
  # This ensures consistency in the database.

  # Normalize email: strip whitespace and convert to lowercase
  # "  User@Example.COM  " → "user@example.com"
  # Why? Prevents duplicate accounts due to case differences
  normalizes :email, with: ->(email) { email.strip.downcase }

  # ===========================================================================
  # INSTANCE METHODS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Check if email has been verified
  # Used to restrict certain features to verified users
  def email_verified?
    email_verified_at.present?
  end

  # Check if user is an admin
  # Used for authorization checks
  def admin?
    role == 'admin'
  end

  private

  # Record the current password digest in history for reuse prevention
  def record_password_history
    PasswordHistory.record_password(self, password_digest)
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# MIGRATION: Create AuthCredentials Table
# =============================================================================
# This table stores user login credentials.
#
# IMPORTANT: This is NOT a "users" table!
# - Auth credentials: email, password hash (lives here, in auth-api)
# - User profile: name, phone, preferences (lives in core-api)
# - Linked by: user_id (UUID) shared between services
#
# This separation is intentional for microservices:
# - Auth-api owns authentication (passwords, tokens)
# - Core-api owns user data (profiles, appointments)
# - Neither needs direct database access to the other

class CreateAuthCredentials < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.0]
  def change
    create_table :auth_credentials, id: :uuid do |t|
      # =======================================================================
      # COLUMNS
      # =======================================================================

      # Email address (used as username)
      # null: false → Required field, can't be blank
      t.string :email, null: false

      # Hashed password (bcrypt output)
      # SECURITY: We NEVER store plain-text passwords
      # bcrypt hash looks like: $2a$12$K4... (60 characters)
      t.string :password_digest, null: false

      # User role for authorization
      # Default 'user' for regular users, 'admin' for administrators
      # Used in JWT payload for role-based access control
      t.string :role, null: false, default: 'user'

      # Email verification timestamp
      # NULL means not verified, timestamp means verified at that time
      # Future feature: require email verification before login
      t.datetime :email_verified_at

      # Rails timestamps: created_at, updated_at
      # Automatically managed by ActiveRecord
      t.timestamps
    end

    # =========================================================================
    # INDEXES
    # =========================================================================

    # Unique index on email (case-insensitive matching happens in model)
    # Why unique? Each email can only have one account
    # Why index? Fast lookups during login (we query by email)
    add_index :auth_credentials, :email, unique: true
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Redis-backed JWT denylist supporting two revocation strategies:
#
# 1. JTI revocation (logout) — stores a specific token ID with TTL equal to
#    the token's remaining lifetime so Redis auto-expires the entry.
#
# 2. Per-user timestamp invalidation (password change / reset) — records the
#    current time for a user; any token whose `iat` is <= that timestamp is
#    rejected. TTL is ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRATION + 1 minute to cover clock skew.
#
# Redis key structure:
#   jti:denylist:<jti>               → '1'  (TTL = remaining token seconds)
#   user:tokens:invalidated:<uid>    → epoch (TTL = ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRATION + 60s)
class TokenDenylist
  JTI_PREFIX = 'jti:denylist:'
  USER_INVALIDATE_PREFIX = 'user:tokens:invalidated:'
  # Keep the user-invalidation key alive slightly longer than the access token
  # so that every token issued before the password change is caught.
  USER_INVALIDATE_TTL = (JWTService::ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRATION + 1.minute).to_i

  class << self
    # Revoke a specific token by JTI. exp is the Unix timestamp from the JWT.
    def revoke!(jti, exp)
      ttl = exp.to_i - Time.current.to_i
      return if ttl <= 0

      redis.setex("#{JTI_PREFIX}#{jti}", ttl, '1')
    end

    def revoked?(jti)
      redis.exists?("#{JTI_PREFIX}#{jti}") == 1
    end

    # Invalidate all outstanding access tokens for a user by snapshotting now.
    def invalidate_user_tokens!(user_id)
      redis.setex(
        "#{USER_INVALIDATE_PREFIX}#{user_id}",
        USER_INVALIDATE_TTL,
        Time.current.to_i.to_s
      )
    end

    # Returns true if this token was issued before the user's invalidation point.
    def user_tokens_invalidated?(user_id, iat)
      invalidated_at = redis.get("#{USER_INVALIDATE_PREFIX}#{user_id}")
      return false unless invalidated_at

      iat.to_i <= invalidated_at.to_i
    end

    private

    def redis
      @redis ||= Redis.new(url: ENV.fetch('REDIS_URL', 'redis://localhost:6379/0'))
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTH CREDENTIAL MODEL
# =============================================================================
# Represents a user's login credentials (email + password).
#
# This is the core model for authentication. It:
# - Stores email and hashed password
# - Validates password requirements (NIST SP 800-63B compliant)
# - Associates with refresh tokens for session management
# - Tracks password history to prevent reuse
#
# SECURITY: Passwords are NEVER stored in plain text.
# We use bcrypt (via has_secure_password) which:
# - Hashes the password with a random salt
# - Uses intentionally slow algorithm (prevents brute-force)
# - Automatically handles comparison securely
class AuthCredential < ApplicationRecord
  # ===========================================================================
  # CONSTANTS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Valid user roles for authorization
  # 'user': Regular patient/customer
  # 'admin': Administrator with elevated privileges
  ROLES = %w[user admin].freeze

  # ===========================================================================
  # SECURE PASSWORD
  # ===========================================================================
  # This Rails macro adds:
  # - password= setter that hashes the password
  # - password_confirmation= setter for confirmation validation
  # - authenticate(password) method for login verification
  # - Validation that password_digest is present
  #
  # Requires 'bcrypt' gem and password_digest column in database
  has_secure_password

  # ===========================================================================
  # ASSOCIATIONS
  # ===========================================================================

  # One user can have multiple refresh tokens (multiple devices/sessions)
  # dependent: :destroy → Delete all tokens when user is deleted
  has_many :refresh_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # Track password history to prevent reuse (NIST requirement)
  has_many :password_histories, dependent: :destroy

  # Password reset tokens for forgot password flow
  has_many :password_reset_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # Email verification tokens
  has_many :email_verification_tokens, dependent: :destroy

  # ===========================================================================
  # VALIDATIONS
  # ===========================================================================
  # Validations run before saving to database.
  # Invalid records are not saved, and errors are added to the object.

  # Email must be present, unique, and valid format
  validates :email,
            presence: true,
            uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false },
            format: { with: URI::MailTo::EMAIL_REGEXP }

  # NIST SP 800-63B compliant password validation
  # Only validate when creating or changing password
  # Note: Use :: prefix to reference top-level PasswordValidator (not AuthCredential::PasswordValidator)
  validates_with ::PasswordValidator, if: -> { new_record? || password.present? }

  # Role must be present and one of the valid roles
  validates :role,
            presence: true,
            inclusion: { in: ROLES }

  # ===========================================================================
  # CALLBACKS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Record password in history after successful save (for reuse prevention)
  after_save :record_password_history, if: :saved_change_to_password_digest?

  # ===========================================================================
  # NORMALIZATIONS (Rails 7.1+)
  # ===========================================================================
  # Normalizations transform attribute values before validation/saving.
  # This ensures consistency in the database.

  # Normalize email: strip whitespace and convert to lowercase
  # "  User@Example.COM  " → "user@example.com"
  # Why? Prevents duplicate accounts due to case differences
  normalizes :email, with: ->(email) { email.strip.downcase }

  # ===========================================================================
  # INSTANCE METHODS
  # ===========================================================================

  # Check if email has been verified
  # Used to restrict certain features to verified users
  def email_verified?
    email_verified_at.present?
  end

  # Check if user is an admin
  # Used for authorization checks
  def admin?
    role == 'admin'
  end

  private

  # Record the current password digest in history for reuse prevention
  def record_password_history
    PasswordHistory.record_password(self, password_digest)
  end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module API
  module V1
    module Admin
      class UsersController < ApplicationController
        before_action :authenticate_request
        before_action :require_admin
        before_action :set_user, only: %i[show deactivate activate]

        # GET /api/v1/admin/users
        def index
          users = AuthCredential.order(created_at: :desc)
          render json: { users: users.map { |u| admin_user_payload(u) } }
        end

        # GET /api/v1/admin/users/:id
        def show
          render json: { user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/deactivate
        def deactivate
          if @user.deactivated?
            render json: { error: 'User is already deactivated' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          if @user.id == @current_user.id
            render json: { error: 'Cannot deactivate your own account' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
            @user.update!(deactivated_at: Time.current)
            @user.refresh_tokens.active.find_each(&:revoke!)
          end

          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(@user.id)
          render json: { message: 'User deactivated successfully', user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/activate
        def activate
          unless @user.deactivated?
            render json: { error: 'User is already active' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          @user.update!(deactivated_at: nil)
          render json: { message: 'User activated successfully', user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        private

        def authenticate_request
          token = request.headers['Authorization']&.split&.last

          if token.blank?
            render json: { error: 'Authorization header missing' }, status: :unauthorized
            return
          end

          result = JWTService.decode(token)

          if result.success?
            @current_user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: result.payload[:sub])
            unless @current_user
              render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :unauthorized
              return
            end

            if @current_user.deactivated?
              render json: { error: 'Account has been deactivated' }, status: :forbidden
              return
            end

            @current_token_jti = result.payload[:jti]
            @current_token_exp = result.payload[:exp]
          else
            render json: { error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
          end
        end

        def require_admin
          return if @current_user&.admin?

          render json: { error: 'Forbidden' }, status: :forbidden
        end

        def set_user
          @user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: params[:id])
          render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :not_found unless @user
        end

        def admin_user_payload(credential)
          {
            id: credential.id,
            email: credential.email,
            role: credential.role,
            email_verified: credential.email_verified?,
            deactivated: credential.deactivated?,
            deactivated_at: credential.deactivated_at,
            created_at: credential.created_at,
            updated_at: credential.updated_at
          }
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

              render json: { error: 'Account has been deactivated' }, status: :forbidden
              return
            end

            @current_token_jti = result.payload[:jti]
            @current_token_exp = result.payload[:exp]
          else
            render json: { error: result.error }, status: :unauthorized
          end
        end

        def require_admin
          return if @current_user&.admin?

          render json: { error: 'Forbidden' }, status: :forbidden
        end

        def set_user
          @user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: params[:id])
          render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :not_found unless @user
        end

        def admin_user_payload(credential)
          {
            id: credential.id,
            email: credential.email,
            role: credential.role,
            email_verified: credential.email_verified?,
            deactivated: credential.deactivated?,
            deactivated_at: credential.deactivated_at,
            created_at: credential.created_at,
            updated_at: credential.updated_at
          }
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# ROUTES.RB - API Endpoint Definitions
# =============================================================================
# This file maps URLs to controller actions.
# Run `bin/rails routes` to see the full routing table.
#
# URL Structure: /api/v1/auth/<action>
# - /api: Namespace for API (vs admin, webhooks, etc.)
# - /v1: API version (allows v2 later without breaking v1 clients)
# - /auth: Authentication resource

Rails.application.routes.draw do
  mount Rswag::Ui::Engine => '/api-docs'
  mount Rswag::Api::Engine => '/api-docs'
  # ===========================================================================
  # HEALTH CHECKS
  # ===========================================================================
  # These endpoints are used by:
  # - Docker/Kubernetes to check if the container is alive
  # - Load balancers to check if the server can handle requests
  # - Monitoring systems to track uptime

  # Basic liveness check: "Is Rails running?"
  # Returns 200 OK if the app is alive
  get 'up', to: 'health#show'

  # ===========================================================================
  # API V1 ROUTES
  # ===========================================================================
  # Namespace creates URL prefix AND module structure:
  # - URL: /api/v1/auth/login
  # - Controller: Api::V1::AuthenticationController#login

  namespace :api do
    namespace :v1 do
      # ========================================================================
      # AUTHENTICATION ENDPOINTS
      # ========================================================================
      # These are the core auth endpoints used by all clients
      # (frontend, mobile, other microservices)

      # POST /api/v1/auth/register
      # Create a new user account
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com", password: "securepass" }
      # Returns: { user: {...}, access_token: "...", refresh_token: "..." }
      post 'auth/register', to: 'authentication#register'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/login
      # Authenticate existing user
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com", password: "securepass" }
      # Returns: { user: {...}, access_token: "...", refresh_token: "..." }
      post 'auth/login', to: 'authentication#login'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/logout
      # Revoke refresh token (invalidate session)
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Body: { refresh_token: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Logged out successfully" }
      post 'auth/logout', to: 'authentication#logout'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
      # Exchange refresh token for new access token
      # Body: { refresh_token: "..." }
      # Returns: { access_token: "...", user: {...} }
      post 'auth/refresh', to: 'authentication#refresh'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/validate
      # Validate an access token (used by other microservices)
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Returns: { valid: true, payload: {...} } or { valid: false, error: "..." }
      # CRITICAL: Core API and Admin call this to verify tokens
      post 'auth/validate', to: 'authentication#validate'

      # GET /api/v1/auth/me
      # Get current authenticated user's info
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Returns: { user: {...} }
      get 'auth/me', to: 'authentication#me'

      # PATCH /api/v1/auth/password
      # Change authenticated user's password
      # Headers: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
      # Body: { current_password: "...", new_password: "...", new_password_confirmation: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Password updated successfully" }
      patch 'auth/password', to: 'authentication#change_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
      # Request password reset email
      # Body: { email: "user@example.com" }
      # Returns: { message: "If an account exists..." } (always 200 for security)
      post 'auth/forgot-password', to: 'authentication#forgot_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
      # Reset password using token from email
      # Body: { token: "...", new_password: "...", new_password_confirmation: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Password reset successfully" }
      post 'auth/reset-password', to: 'authentication#reset_password'

      # POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email
      # Verify email address using token from verification email
      # Body: { token: "..." }
      # Returns: { message: "Email verified successfully" }
      post 'auth/verify-email', to: 'authentication#verify_email'
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTH CREDENTIAL FACTORY
# =============================================================================
# FactoryBot factory for creating test AuthCredential records.
#
# Usage:
#   create(:auth_credential)                    # Basic user
#   create(:auth_credential, :admin)            # Admin user
#   create(:auth_credential, :verified)         # Email verified user
#   create(:auth_credential, email: 'custom@example.com')  # Custom email
#   build(:auth_credential)                     # Build without saving

FactoryBot.define do
  factory :auth_credential do
    # Sequence generates unique emails: user1@example.com, user2@example.com, ...
    sequence(:email) { |n| "user#{n}@example.com" }

    # Default password (meets NIST minimum 14 character requirement)
    password { 'securepassword14' }

# frozen_string_literal: true

require 'swagger_helper'

# rubocop:disable RSpec/EmptyExampleGroup, RSpec/VariableName
RSpec.describe 'Admin Users API', type: :request do
  let(:admin) { create(:auth_credential, :admin) }
  let(:admin_token) { JWTService.generate_access_token(admin) }
  let(:Authorization) { "Bearer #{admin_token}" }

  # ===========================================================================
  # GET /api/v1/admin/users
  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users' do
    get 'List all users' do
      tags 'Admin - Users'
      security [bearer_auth: []]
      produces 'application/json'

      response '200', 'Users listed' do
        schema type: :object,
               properties: {
                 users: {
                   type: :array,
                   items: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' }
                 }
               }

        before { create_list(:auth_credential, 2) }
        run_test!
      end

      response '401', 'Missing or invalid token' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:Authorization) { nil }
        run_test!
      end

      response '403', 'Not an admin' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:regular_user) { create(:auth_credential) }
        let(:Authorization) { "Bearer #{JWTService.generate_access_token(regular_user)}" }
        run_test!
      end
    end
  end

  # ===========================================================================
  # GET /api/v1/admin/users/:id
  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users/{id}' do
    parameter name: :id, in: :path, type: :string, format: :uuid

    get 'Get a user' do
      tags 'Admin - Users'
      security [bearer_auth: []]
      produces 'application/json'

      response '200', 'User found' do
        schema type: :object,
               properties: {
                 user: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' }
               }

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential) }
        let(:id) { target.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '404', 'User not found' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:id) { '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' }
        run_test!
      end
    end
  end

  # ===========================================================================
  # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/deactivate
  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/deactivate' do
    parameter name: :id, in: :path, type: :string, format: :uuid

    patch 'Deactivate a user' do
      tags 'Admin - Users'
      security [bearer_auth: []]
      produces 'application/json'

      response '200', 'User deactivated' do
        schema type: :object,
               properties: {
                 message: { type: :string },
                 user: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' }
               }

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential) }
        let(:id) { target.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '422', 'Already deactivated or self-deactivation' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:id) { admin.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '404', 'User not found' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:id) { '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' }
        run_test!
      end
    end
  end

  # ===========================================================================
  # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/activate
  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/activate' do
    parameter name: :id, in: :path, type: :string, format: :uuid

    patch 'Activate a user' do
      tags 'Admin - Users'
      security [bearer_auth: []]
      produces 'application/json'

      response '200', 'User activated' do
        schema type: :object,
               properties: {
                 message: { type: :string },
                 user: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' }
               }

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential, :deactivated) }
        let(:id) { target.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '422', 'User is already active' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential) }
        let(:id) { target.id }
        run_test!
      end
    end
  end
end

message.content: how is represented a user model

content.text:
Split across two services by design (noted in the migration comment):

**`auth-api` — `AuthCredential`** (what exists)
```
id               uuid  PK
email            string  unique
password_digest  string  (bcrypt)
role             string  user|admin
email_verified_at datetime  nullable
deactivated_at   datetime  nullable
created_at / updated_at
```
Owns: authentication, sessions, tokens.

**`core-api` — `User`** (scaffolded, not built yet)
Would own: name, phone, profile data, appointments, etc.

The two are linked by the same `id` UUID — `auth-api` issues the UUID, `core-api` stores a matching record. No direct DB cross-access; services communicate via JWT (`sub` claim = user UUID) or API calls.

So right now "a user" = just an `AuthCredential`. The profile half doesn't exist yet.

message.content: why does the model is represented that way ?

lastPrompt: why does the model is represented that way ?

content.text:
Intentional microservices design. The migration comment explains it directly:

> "This is NOT a 'users' table! Auth credentials live here, user profiles live in core-api. Linked by UUID shared between services."

**Why:**

- **Separation of concerns** — `auth-api` owns one thing: "can you prove you are who you say you are." It doesn't care if your name is Bob or if you have 3 appointments.
- **Independent scaling** — auth gets hit on every request (token validation). Profile data gets hit less often. Two separate services scale independently.
- **Security boundary** — if `core-api` gets compromised, the attacker doesn't have password hashes. Credentials are isolated.
- **Independent deployment** — you can update the profile schema in `core-api` without touching auth.

**The link:**

```
auth-api: AuthCredential.id = UUID  ──┐
                                       │ same UUID
core-api: User.id            = UUID  ──┘
```

Other services trust the JWT `sub` claim (= that UUID) to know who the user is, without querying auth-api's DB directly.

It's a standard pattern for auth microservices. The tradeoff: getting a "full user" requires joining data from two services instead of one query.

message.content: ok I understand but when an AuthCredential is created how does it creates a user in coreapi

content.text:
It doesn't. Core-api isn't built yet — just an empty scaffold.

When core-api gets built, two common patterns to wire it up:

**Option A — Event-driven (fits your stack)**
`auth-api` already has RabbitMQ + `EventPublisher`. On register, publish `user.created` event. Core-api consumes it and creates the `User` record. Same pattern as `email.password_reset`.

**Option B — Sync HTTP call**
After `AuthCredential` saves, `auth-api` calls core-api's REST API to create the user. Simpler but creates tight coupling + failure risk.

**Option A is the right choice here** — you already have the broker, the publisher, and a consumer pattern in `notification-service` to copy from. Core-api just needs a consumer for `user.created`.

When you build core-api, that's the first thing to wire up.
